solutions.section.outcome: What you get at the output
solutions.page.painLead: Typical bottlenecks that cost money and time.
solutions.page.automationLead: Modules and processes we connect into a single loop for your model.
solutions.page.outcomeLead: What changes for your team and in the numbers after rollout.
solutions.page.scenariosLead: What it looks like in real cycles—from lead to payment and reporting.
solutions.page.moreSolutionsLead: The same platform—adapted to your business model and growth path.
solutions.retail.title: Retail, Stores & Franchises
solutions.retail.lead: Warehouses and storefront, applications from social networks, repeated sales and cash register control — in one system instead of tables.
solutions.retail.b1: Accounting for balances and orders between points without "black holes".
solutions.retail.b2: Sales funnel and tasks for the shift: who calls back to whom and what is in the work.
solutions.retail.b3: Contract and invoice templates, payment statuses, and reminders.
solutions.retail.b4: Reports on revenue and conversions by channels and points.
solutions.retail.forWho: Retail networks, several points and channels (offline, delivery, social networks), franchise and e-commerce. It is suitable when sales go in parallel, and accounting and responsibility are smeared.
solutions.retail.pain1: Balances and sales in different files or in the mind of managers: it is not clear what is real in the warehouse and what to order to the supplier.
solutions.retail.pain2: Applications from Instagram, marketplaces and cash desk do not converge in one customer card — duplicates, lost leads, disputes about discounts.
solutions.retail.pain3: It is difficult for the owner to see the margin and turnover by points and channels without manual summaries.
solutions.retail.out1: A single funnel and tasks: from the first contact to payment, with clear roles on the shift and in the office.
solutions.retail.out2: Warehouse and movement between points in the same contour; less re-sort and "black holes".
solutions.retail.out3: Revenue, conversion and workload reports — for assortment and marketing solutions.
solutions.retail.ex1: The client wrote in Direct — the manager conducts the transaction in the CRM, the payment is fixed, the warehouse prepares the order according to the invoice.
solutions.retail.ex2: The franchisee sees the plan/fact at its point; the head office — the aggregated picture over the network and the debtor.
solutions.retail.ex3: The movement of goods between stores is formalized with acceptance tasks and responsible persons on each side.
solutions.services.title: Services, studios and project teams
solutions.services.lead: Sales of services are deadlines, milestones and responsible persons. Taska links the application, quotation, contract and execution in one thread.
solutions.services.b1: Project cards with milestones, budget and files.
solutions.services.b2: Roles and rights: who sees finances, who sees only tasks.
solutions.services.b3: Automatic tasks after the stages: approval, payment, start of work.
solutions.services.b4: Dashboard for team loading and project margin.
solutions.services.forWho: Agencies, studios, IT and consulting, construction and service companies, where money is tied up at the stages of the project, deadlines and the workload of people.
solutions.services.pain1: Estimates and facts separate: the contract is signed, and additional works and approvals live in correspondence, not in the system.
solutions.services.pain2: It is unclear who bottleneck is now: sales, production or finance — there is no single task board and funnel.
solutions.services.pain3: The manager does not see the margin on projects and the cash forecast without unloading from several tables.
solutions.services.out1: The project as a single entity: stages, budget, files, responsible persons — without losing context between departments.
solutions.services.out2: Automatic tasks when changing the stage: coordination, invoice, start of work, control of deadlines.
solutions.services.out3: Transparent team workload and financial result for projects for management decisions.
solutions.services.ex1: A new lead from the site enters the funnel; after qualification, a project is created with a template of stages for the type of service.
solutions.services.ex2: Accounting sees payments and acts; production — only its tasks and deadlines without access to other people's contracts.
solutions.services.ex3: Closing the stage triggers an invoice to the client and a reminder of payment; the delay is highlighted to the manager.
solutions.manufacturing.title: Manufacturing & Workshops
solutions.manufacturing.lead: Release planning, raw materials, scrap and shipments — when Excel fails, processes need to be digitized.
solutions.manufacturing.b2: Control of stages and responsible persons at each step.
solutions.manufacturing.b3: Connection with procurement and warehouse (if necessary — integration).
solutions.manufacturing.b4: Clear reports for the manager: deadlines, bottlenecks, release.
solutions.manufacturing.forWho: Workshops, small and medium-sized production, assembly lines, where the queue of orders, raw materials, scrap and shipment are important.
solutions.manufacturing.pain1: Orders and deadlines in Excel: the shop does not know the priorities, supply — what is really needed for tomorrow.
solutions.manufacturing.pain2: Marriage and alterations are not tied to the stages and responsible ones — the same mistakes are repeated.
solutions.manufacturing.pain3: It is difficult for a manager to understand where the bottleneck is: in procurement, on the line or on shipment.
solutions.manufacturing.out1: Register of production orders with queue and statuses by workshop and sites.
solutions.manufacturing.out2: Tasks and notifications at each crossing: from workpiece to QCD and shipment.
solutions.manufacturing.out3: Reports on deadlines, downtime and release — to plan loading and procurement.
solutions.manufacturing.ex1: A large order is divided into batches; each site receives tasks and marks readiness.
solutions.manufacturing.ex2: During marriage, a review is created with a responsible person and a task for corrective actions.
solutions.manufacturing.ex3: Sourcing sees material requisitions from the production plan, not from verbal formulations.
solutions.logistics.title: Logistics, Warehouses & Shipping
solutions.logistics.lead: The statuses of shipments, routes and responsible persons should be visible without calls "where is the cargo". Taska fixes the chain and alerts.
solutions.logistics.b1: Tracking the status of deliveries and tasks for each point.
solutions.logistics.b2: Unified database of counterparties and contracts.
solutions.logistics.b3: Notifications in Telegram about deadlines and delays.
solutions.logistics.forWho: Warehouses, shipping, 3PL, import/export and companies with order — shipment — return chain.
solutions.logistics.pain1: The statuses of cargoes are scattered across chats and calls — the client and the office do not see the same truth.
solutions.logistics.pain2: Supply disruptions pop up at the last moment; penalties and downtime are not tracked for reasons.
solutions.logistics.pain3: It is difficult to summarize the actual timing and cost of the route for the revision of counterparties and tariffs.
solutions.logistics.out1: A single picture of requests, shipments and delays with tasks for those responsible at each point.
solutions.logistics.out2: Alerts in Telegram about window breaks and deviations from the plan — without "where is the cargo?" in general chats.
solutions.logistics.out3: Warehouse turnover and downtime analytics — for stock and partner decisions.
solutions.logistics.ex1: The customer issued the delivery — the logistician sees the route, the warehouse prepares the shipment, the driver marks the status.
solutions.logistics.ex2: When there is a delay at the border, a task is automatically created for the responsible person and a notification is sent to the client.
solutions.logistics.ex3: Return of goods is formalized as a process with acceptance at the warehouse and communication with finances.
solutions.wholesale.title: Wholesale, Distribution & Dealers
solutions.wholesale.lead: When there are a lot of customers, it is important to keep prices, delays, shipments and returns in one system, and not in correspondence.
solutions.wholesale.b1: History of orders and conditions for each counterparty.
solutions.wholesale.b2: Funnel of large transactions and payment control.
solutions.wholesale.b3: Tasks for managers: shipment, documents, reconciliations.
solutions.wholesale.b4: Analytics by region, SKU and managers.
solutions.wholesale.forWho: Wholesalers, distributors, dealer networks and B2B sales with deferrals, prices and regional managers.
solutions.wholesale.pain1: The conditions for counterparties (price, deferral, limit) are smeared on files and correspondence.
solutions.wholesale.pain2: Large transactions and small wholesale live in different modes — the funnel and the cash desk forecast do not converge.
solutions.wholesale.pain3: It is difficult to see the manager's contribution and margin by region/SKU without manual analytics.
solutions.wholesale.out1: History of orders, conditions and accounts receivable for each counterparty in one card.
solutions.wholesale.out2: Funnel of large transactions with payment control and tasks for shipment and documents.
solutions.wholesale.out3: Dashboards by region, nomenclature and sales team — for bonuses and pricing.
solutions.wholesale.ex1: New dealer: Credit limit approval, price list and first order go through an agreed process.
solutions.wholesale.ex2: The field manager sees only his clients and the plan; the manager — a summary of the network.
solutions.wholesale.ex3: The SKU promotion triggers agreed prices and tasks for the warehouse and sales department.
solutions.construction.title: Construction, general contracting and subcontracting
solutions.construction.lead: Estimates, certificates, work schedules and approvals — when there are many projects, deadlines and margins are lost without a single contour.
solutions.construction.b1: Projects and facilities with milestones, estimates and files.
solutions.construction.b2: Tasks for VET, supply and crews with deadlines.
solutions.construction.b3: Link with finances: advances, certificates, customer accounts receivable.
solutions.construction.b4: Reports on the workload of teams and deviations from the schedule.
solutions.construction.forWho: General contractors, developers, design organizations and subcontracting, where the timing of stages, document flow and margin control are important.
solutions.construction.pain1: The schedule of work and the fact at the site differ: in the office one version, on the site another.
solutions.construction.pain2: Acts, additional messages and correspondence in messengers — it is difficult to collect a complete picture of the object.
solutions.construction.pain3: The head does not see bottlenecks: where the supply is delayed, where the crew is idle.
solutions.construction.out1: Object and stages in one system: statuses, responsible persons and files without losing context.
solutions.construction.out2: Tasks and notifications on the deadlines for WEP, deliveries and acceptance are less disruptions and "forgotten" acts.
solutions.construction.out3: Transparent margin and contract receivables are the basis for pricing and resource decisions.
solutions.construction.ex1: New Facility: Stage Template, Assignment of Foreman and Supply, Tolerance Checklist.
solutions.construction.ex2: Delay in delivery of material is an automatic task for the responsible person and correction of dependent works.
solutions.construction.ex3: Closing the stage triggers the approval of the certificate and invoicing to the customer.
solutions.healthcare.title: Clinics, dentistry, diagnostics
solutions.healthcare.lead: The schedule, applications, approvals and control of the service — when there are a lot of patients, the processes should be predictable.
solutions.healthcare.b1: A single funnel for recording and appeals.
solutions.healthcare.b2: Tasks for administrators, doctors and equipment.
solutions.healthcare.b3: Reminders to customers and internal quality checklists.
solutions.healthcare.b4: Reports on loading accounts and conversion of requests.
solutions.healthcare.forWho: Private clinics, dentists, diagnostic centers and networks where staff records, service and discipline are important.
solutions.healthcare.pain1: Records and transfers live in tables and chats — slot conflicts and not calling the client.
solutions.healthcare.pain2: There is no single place where you can see who is responsible for equipment, consumables and staff training.
solutions.healthcare.pain3: It is difficult for management to measure conversion of hits and loading without manual summaries.
solutions.healthcare.out1: The flow from the first contact to the visit with tasks and statuses at each step.
solutions.healthcare.out2: Internal processes: procurement, maintenance of offices, onboarding — with responsibilities and deadlines.
solutions.healthcare.out3: Clear metrics for records, cancellations and revenue — for marketing and operational management.
solutions.healthcare.ex1: The client left a request on the site — the manager contacts, the slot is fixed, the reminder leaves automatically.
solutions.healthcare.ex2: Consumables have run out — a procurement task with a priority and a deadline.
solutions.healthcare.ex3: New doctor: checklist of access, training and first shifts with supervisor control.
solutions.hospitality.title: Restaurants, hotels, catering
solutions.hospitality.lead: Shifts, requests for banquets, purchases and control of the service — when there are a lot of guests, chaos in chats is expensive.
solutions.hospitality.b1: Shift tasks: hall, kitchen, bar, delivery.
solutions.hospitality.b2: Large orders and events as projects with stages.
solutions.hospitality.b3: Connection with finances: prepayments, certificates, suppliers.
solutions.hospitality.b4: Reports on incidents, deadlines and loading points.
solutions.hospitality.forWho: Restaurants, cafes, hotels, catering and food delivery — where shifts, service standards and consistency of cuisine and hall are important.
solutions.hospitality.pain1: Guest issues and internal tasks are lost in general chats — there is no responsible person and no deadline.
solutions.hospitality.pain2: Large banquets and corporate parties require a dozen approvals without a single tracker.
solutions.hospitality.pain3: The owner does not see repeated failures: kitchen delays, errors in orders, complaints.
solutions.hospitality.out1: A single task board for the shift and event projects with transparent roles.
solutions.hospitality.out2: Process templates for procurement, acceptance and inventory — less manual control.
solutions.hospitality.out3: Incident and standards metrics are the basis for training and bonuses.
solutions.hospitality.ex1: A guest's complaint turns into a task with the responsibility and control of closing on the same day.
solutions.hospitality.ex2: Corporate party for 200 people: stages from quotation to settlement with suppliers and kitchen.
solutions.hospitality.ex3: Inventory: checklist by zones, deadlines and confirmation of the manager.
